ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the rendering event */,
  "fps" REAL /* Frames per second (FPS) achieved during the rendering */,
  "render_time_ms" REAL /* Render time in milliseconds */,
  "triangles" INTEGER /* Number of triangles processed during the rendering */,
  "vertices" INTEGER /* Number of vertices processed during the rendering */,
  "distance" INTEGER /* Distance from the camera or observer */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application being tested */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device used for testing */,
  "rendering_engine" TEXT /* Rendering engine used for the test */,
  "rendering_resolution" TEXT /* Resolution used for the test */,
  "rendering_quality" TEXT /* Quality settings used for the test */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage during the test */,
  "gpu_usage" REAL /* GPU usage during the test */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage during the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_rendering_quality FOREIGN KEY ("rendering_quality") REFERENCES rendering_qualities ("quality_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_rendering_engine FOREIGN KEY ("rendering_engine") REFERENCES rendering_engines ("engine_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_rendering_resolution FOREIGN KEY ("rendering_resolution") REFERENCES rendering_resolutions ("resolution_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "app_version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the application */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the application */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., PC, Mobile, Console) */,
  "device_os" TEXT /* Operating system of the device */,
  "graphics_card" TEXT /* Graphics card used in the device */,
  "cpu_model" TEXT /* CPU model used in the device */,
  "ram_size" INTEGER /* RAM size of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, QA engineer, manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the metric */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rendering_engines" (
  "engine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rendering engine */,
  "engine_name" TEXT /* Name of the rendering engine */,
  "engine_version" TEXT /* Version of the rendering engine */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rendering_resolutions" (
  "resolution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rendering resolution */,
  "resolution_name" TEXT /* Name of the rendering resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resolution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rendering_qualities" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rendering quality */,
  "quality_name" TEXT /* Name of the rendering quality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each performance metric entry
Frames per second (FPS) achieved during the rendering
Number of triangles processed during the rendering
Number of vertices processed during the rendering
Distance from the camera or observer
Reference to the application being tested
Reference to the device used for testing
Rendering engine used for the test
Quality settings used for the test
Type of the device (e.g., PC, Mobile, Console)
Graphics card used in the device
CPU model used in the device
Role of the user (e.g., developer, QA engineer, manager)

ID of the performance metric being accessed
ID of the user accessing the metric
Date when the metric was accessed
Duration of the access in seconds

Unique identifier for each rendering quality
